explicit_gaps.description: The corpus defines retention classes, expiry basis, and erasure blockers, but does not publish one canonical per-artifact duration matrix.
Threat classes are normatively defined in prose in the runtime hardening contract, but no dedicated schema-backed enum exists in the current corpus.
The corpus forbids stored authority login credentials by design, so there is no dedicated persisted schema artifact for that credential class.
Restore privacy reconciliation states are defined, but the corpus does not separately publish a per-surface reopen matrix for each operator and customer route family.

rows.encryption_or_protection_control: anti-CSRF token, secure cookie flags, CSP, clickjacking defenses
rows.erasure_behavior: Not applicable to payload erasure; applies to browser-origin request validity.
rows.legal_hold_behavior: Not stateful beyond audit evidence of revocation and challenge changes.
notes: Browser-origin write actions require authenticated session plus anti-CSRF posture.
rows.source_heading_or_logical_block: 4. Browser, native-client, API, and transport hardening
rows.survivability_or_fidelity_formula: cookie-based browser sessions require anti-CSRF protection and secure cookie flags.

rows.erasure_behavior: Ingress lineage survives retries and recovery; it is not reconstructed from transport memory.
rows.legal_hold_behavior: Quarantine, dedupe, and reconciliation ownership remain explicit under hold.
notes: A lone authority_reference match remains quarantined, not trusted as legal correlation.
rows.survivability_or_fidelity_formula: No authority callback or recovery payload mutates legal state before authenticated ingress, dedupe, and request-lineage correlation complete.

rows.encryption_or_protection_control: cache_isolation_contract, delivery binding hash, temp artifact purge policy
rows.erasure_behavior: Broader variants purge immediately on access or masking narrowing.
rows.legal_hold_behavior: Hold does not permit reuse across tenants, masking posture, or route drift.
notes: Cache identity is a security boundary across browser, portal, governance, and native surfaces.
rows.survivability_or_fidelity_formula: exact security-context match only; purge-or-reject on tenant, principal, session, access, masking, route, projection-version, or preview drift.

rows.encryption_or_protection_control: redaction, masked values, summary-first telemetry, export policy inheritance
rows.erasure_behavior: Diagnostic tooling may not reintroduce data hidden by privacy or masking policy.
rows.legal_hold_behavior: Audit may preserve reason and lineage minimums without exposing unnecessary raw values.
notes: Exports inherit masking/export policy and cannot bypass it through direct object-store URLs.
rows.source_heading_or_logical_block: 6. Data protection, privacy, and cache safety
rows.survivability_or_fidelity_formula: logs redact secrets, raw tokens, government identifiers, and full regulated payload bodies.

rows.canonical_store_or_boundary: native scene and cursor hydration boundary
rows.erasure_behavior: Purge clears structured cache, resume metadata, scene-restoration payloads, NSUserActivity, preview caches, temp exports, and local search indices.
rows.legal_hold_behavior: Hold does not bypass first-paint legality or mutation gate after cache-only restore.
notes: Native speed may reuse compatible state, but may not outrun legality.

rows.encryption_or_protection_control: signed and notarized macOS build with hardened runtime and least-privilege entitlements
rows.erasure_behavior: Desktop-only evidence clears when the desktop target is absent, preventing overclaiming.
rows.legal_hold_behavior: Release evidence preserves desktop hardening posture whenever the macOS target ships.
notes: The desktop client is blocked from production without hardening evidence.
rows.source_heading_or_logical_block: 4. Browser, native-client, API, and transport hardening
rows.survivability_or_fidelity_formula: no production desktop client without signature, notarization, and hardened-runtime policy compliance

rows.canonical_store_or_boundary: object store and regulated payload boundary
rows.erasure_behavior: Erased or minimized artifacts may retain lawful encrypted refs or hashes, not raw content.
rows.legal_hold_behavior: Hold preserves encrypted artifacts without downgrading the encryption boundary.
notes: Applies to sensitive client and authority data.
rows.survivability_or_fidelity_formula: object payload encryption uses per-tenant or per-sensitivity envelope keys rooted in KMS/HSM master keys.

rows.encryption_or_protection_control: checkpoint refs, restore drill refs, audit continuity, replay/enquiry limitation safety
rows.erasure_behavior: Resurrected data opens compensating re-erasure before normal access reopens.
rows.legal_hold_behavior: Blocked legal hold, proof preservation, and authority ambiguity states keep reopened access limited.
notes: Restore reopening is blocked until privacy reconciliation proves lawful access posture.
rows.survivability_or_fidelity_formula: Restore evidence is not lawful production evidence until privacy_reconciliation_state is reconciled.

rows.canonical_store_or_boundary: error, remediation, and gate integration boundary
rows.erasure_behavior: Any material retention condition produces correlated gate, error, and audit evidence.
rows.legal_hold_behavior: Legal hold preventing erasure surfaces as typed blocking effect such as BLOCKS_ERASURE.
notes: Retention posture is a gate input, not a UI warning.
rows.survivability_or_fidelity_formula: RETENTION_EVIDENCE_GATE consumes evidence availability, limitation, and erased-path posture directly.

rows.encryption_or_protection_control: typed limitation notes, omission entries, and present-limited truth
rows.erasure_behavior: Explanation AVAILABLE posture is forbidden when decisive support no longer fully survives.
rows.legal_hold_behavior: Hold does not permit silent absence; limitations remain explicit.
notes: Lawful minimization is not permission for silent ambiguity.
rows.survivability_or_fidelity_formula: silent_ambiguity(o) must remain zero for decisive support.

rows.encryption_or_protection_control: schema-validated retention tag and artifact lifecycle objects
rows.erasure_behavior: Fail closed on unresolved hold, statutory minimum, proof-preservation, or authority ambiguity blockers.
rows.legal_hold_behavior: Hold state, refs, and chronology are serialized on canonical retention objects.
notes: Retention state changes remain typed state, not passive prose.
rows.survivability_or_fidelity_formula: RetentionTag and ArtifactRetention serialize the lifecycle basis that later gates consume.

rows.encryption_or_protection_control: SecretVersion schema, attestation refs, historical read window, cutover chronology
rows.erasure_behavior: No key or secret rotation is legal when attestation or version binding is unknown.
rows.legal_hold_behavior: Rotation history remains serialized even after retirement or revocation.
notes: Newly promoted environments require secret-rotation attestation.
rows.survivability_or_fidelity_formula: key and secret rotation shall fail closed if attestation or version binding is unknown.

rows.canonical_store_or_boundary: northbound session and command control plane
rows.encryption_or_protection_control: short-lived ActorSession records and server-side validation
rows.erasure_behavior: Revocation blocks future command acceptance until re-authentication rather than relying on client-held tokens.
rows.legal_hold_behavior: Session audit lineage remains queryable for incident and compliance review.
notes: No write action relies only on a client-held token.
rows.survivability_or_fidelity_formula: Every command validates against current actor/session state; stale commands are rejected.

rows.erasure_behavior: Promotion evidence is replayable from durable artifacts, not dashboards or operator memory.
rows.legal_hold_behavior: Release evidence remains durable and candidate-bound for later scrutiny.
notes: Promotion evidence cannot be assembled from stale green runs or mixed candidate tuples.
rows.survivability_or_fidelity_formula: signed BuildArtifact outputs, SBOM, vulnerability scanning, provenance attestation, and candidate-bound release evidence

rows.canonical_store_or_boundary: connector, OCR, authority gateway network boundary
rows.encryption_or_protection_control: least-privilege network egress policy and authenticated service identity
rows.erasure_behavior: Not an erasure workflow control; prevents unsafe ingress/egress paths.
rows.legal_hold_behavior: Not retention-stateful; applies to transport reachability and external fetch control.
notes: Zero trust applies between service boundaries as well as to external connectors.
rows.survivability_or_fidelity_formula: external fetchers use explicit allowlists and SSRF-resistant URL validation.

rows.canonical_store_or_boundary: session challenge state and approval boundary
rows.encryption_or_protection_control: MFA/step-up, session rotation, revocation audit, and bound cursor invalidation
rows.erasure_behavior: Pre-step-up commands and cursors cannot replay blindly after step-up completion.
rows.legal_hold_behavior: Step-up completion and revocation remain auditable.
notes: Applies to browser, native, and authority-sensitive flows.
rows.survivability_or_fidelity_formula: session rotation after privilege elevation or step-up completion

rows.canonical_store_or_boundary: governed token vault or secret store
rows.encryption_or_protection_control: vault isolation, token_binding_ref, binding_lineage_ref, access_binding_hash, policy_snapshot_hash
rows.erasure_behavior: Raw tokens never persist in queues, read models, or general logs; only opaque refs survive.
rows.legal_hold_behavior: Token lineage and rotation evidence remain auditable; hold does not justify broader token exposure.
notes: No raw authority token exists outside the vault boundary.
rows.survivability_or_fidelity_formula: queued authority mutation revalidates usable token version against persisted binding_lineage_ref immediately before send.
